rationales.hedonism: She spent Sunday lesson planning and immediately agreed to a fourth custom homework request, while brushing off her own eating and sleep, showing self-denial rather than present comfort.
rationales.conformity: She 'said yes before I even finished reading' the parent's email, showing automatic compliance with others' expectations even when it adds to her load.
rationales.benevolence: She keeps taking on extra worksheets for families and tells her mother not to worry because of Dad's surgery, prioritizing other people's needs over her own.
She responded gently when Aiden was wrong and says building a room where a struggling child can 'trust the room enough to risk it' is 'the whole point' for her.

rationales.self_direction: She says she is 'scared she'll go to Principal Davis' and chose the easier path over 'standing up for what I actually think is right for the classroom.'
rationales.hedonism: She is 'drafting a custom enrichment packet at 9pm' for a child who does not need it, sacrificing rest to appease a demanding parent.
rationales.conformity: She 'nodded through the whole thing,' smiled, and agreed to produce extra work mainly because the mother is 'loud,' showing compliance to avoid conflict.
rationales.benevolence: She admits eight students 'reading below benchmark' need her attention more, yet diverts time away from them to satisfy one forceful parent.

rationales.self_direction: She stayed silent about a mandate that will cost 'two instruction days' even though she and Lisa are both frustrated, suppressing her own judgment instead of acting on it.
rationales.conformity: The whole staff 'nodded along' and she 'didn't say anything either,' going along with the group despite private disagreement.

rationales.self_direction: She says she 'heard myself saying sure' and admits she does not know how to say no even when she did not want the extra responsibility.
rationales.hedonism: She keeps adding duties while already stretched thin, and even her mother's 'you can't pour from an empty cup' highlights ongoing neglect of her own limits.
rationales.conformity: She accepted the workshop because 'everyone turned and smiled' and she would feel like she was not 'pulling my weight' if she refused.

rationales.benevolence: A student's drawing of 'me and him and the classroom hamster' means enough to carry her day, showing that caring bonds with her students matter deeply to her.

rationales.hedonism: Her Sunday is dominated by 'Grading. Always grading,' and even dinner and a movie barely register before she falls asleep on the couch.
rationales.achievement: She spends her weekend grading and preparing for parent conferences, keeping up professional responsibilities despite fatigue.

rationales.self_direction: She admits 'I know I should have held my ground' but abandoned her judgment in the moment, acting against what she believed was needed.
rationales.conformity: She 'backpedaled so fast' and reassured Eli's dad to avoid the discomfort of conflict in the conference room.
rationales.benevolence: She recognizes that 'staying quiet means Eli doesn't get the help he needs until someone else brings it up,' so her choice failed to protect the child who needed support.

rationales.hedonism: She took a walk, enjoyed that it 'felt like a Saturday for once,' and made a brunch plan that was 'the first social plan' in weeks.

rationales.benevolence: She frames herself around the idea that 'the kids need someone who shows up' and 'the parents need someone who listens,' centering care for the people around her.

rationales.hedonism: He ate leftover dal 'at my desk' without even heating it and is so depleted that even relaxing afterward feels out of reach.
Even during a genuinely nice balcony chai break, he immediately starts calculating timelines and feels he does not 'deserve twenty minutes of doing nothing.'
He knew a warm drink or 'something comforting' would help, but refused it because he translated caring for himself into 'you're being weak,' choosing misery over relief.
He skipped 'live music, good food' and a night he 'would have loved,' then ended up alone eating 'cold chapati at midnight' after doing the responsible thing.
rationales.achievement: He stayed in to rework a pitch deck because 'the pitch is important' and finished it by midnight, prioritizing deliverable quality over leisure.

rationales.hedonism: He briefly enjoyed 'the chai was good' and 'the afternoon light,' then immediately treated that good feeling like something wrong and forced himself back to the spreadsheet.
He made egg curry, 'ate slowly for once,' and explicitly reflects that small pleasures like cooking and eating properly matter and should not feel like stolen time.
He cannot enjoy good food or downtime without work intruding, saying the biryani 'deserved better attention' and feeling guilty about 'the in-between moments' between work and home.
